MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE AND MARCO HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (RETALIATION) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES A LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASON FOR THE TRANSFER AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE OF PRETEXT. FURTHER, DEFENDANT HERNANDEZ CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION AS A MATTER OF LAW. JONES V. THE LODGE AT TORREY PINES PARTNERSHIP (2008) 42 CAL.4TH 1158, 1173.
DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES INTENTIONAL CONDUCT AND PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE THE FEHA CAUSE OF ACTION. OTHERWISE, THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION BAR WOULD APPLY.
THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO THE 5TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. IF DEFENDANT SEEKS TO VACATE THE DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE AND HAVE A DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE ENTERED, THEN DEFENDANT SHOULD FILE A FORMAL MOTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
